meta.title: Kanazawa City Iouzan Ski Resort
meta.description: ~30 minutes from Kanazawa Station—a half-day hub linking snow play and Yuwa Onsen for international visitors.

hero.eyebrow: Kanazawa, Ishikawa · Mt. Iou · Gateway city
hero.title:
Kanazawa City
Iouzan Ski Resort
hero.tagline: ~30 minutes from Kanazawa Station—a half-day hub linking snow play and Yuwa Onsen for international visitors.

live.subtitle: Check Kanazawa City sports portal
live.updatedAt: Confirm snow & lifts before you go

lift2.label: Courses
lift2.note: Intermediate runs closed for now · check city site

night.label: Slope food
night.note: Route guests to nearby food & onsen

sanctuary.title:
A snow-play backyard
for a tourism city.
sanctuary.lead: A municipal hill inside Iouzan Prefectural Natural Park. Gentle slopes suit children and first-time snow visitors—often uncrowded. Less a freeride destination than a half-day snow add-on to Kanazawa sightseeing.
specs: ~30 min by car from Kanazawa Station
sanctuary.noticeBody: Intermediate and advanced course groups are closed for the foreseeable future per city policy. Confirm lift operation, snow, and fees on the Kanazawa City sports portal (kanazawa-sports.jp) before you travel.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Snow fields and mountain views toward Kanazawa—a snow-play base near a major tourism city

primary.title: Sledding and beginner slopes for international visitors
primary.lead: Many Asian international visitors want snowmen, sledding, and photos more than hardcore skiing. With upper runs closed, repositioning as a Kanazawa Snow Experience for families and overseas FIT is a strategic opportunity.
primary.imgAlt: Sledding and gentle slopes—an international snow-play experience

secondary.title: Short runs, longer trip value
secondary.lead: The weakness of short vertical is offset by a half-day loop: Yuwa Onsen, Edo Village, and Morinosato gyoza—raising spend across the whole area.

bundle.title:
Low-cost municipal
snow play.
bundle.lead: Fees and season dates vary with snow conditions—check the official portal. The strategy report also proposes visitor packages bundling sleds, boots, and wear (e.g. ¥5,000).
bundle.imgAlt: Parking and snow-play zone—500 free spaces at a municipal hill

wellness.title:
Afternoon in
Yuwa Onsen.
wellness.lead: Yuwa Onsen—1,300 years of history. Shirasagi-no-yu public bath and Kanazawa Yuwaku Edo Village turn the lack of slope dining into a regional circuit strength.
wellness.leadStation: ~15 minutes by car from Iouzan to Yuwa—the core of the Snow & Onsen Kanazawa half-day trip.
wellness.imgAlt: Snow-view bath at Yuwa Onsen—classic après snow play

tour.title:
Add half a day of snow
to Kanazawa sightseeing.
tour.lead: Kenrokuen and Higashi Chaya in the morning plan? Add Iouzan snow play, Morinosato gyoza for lunch, then Edo Village and Shirasagi-no-yu in the afternoon.

ecosystem.title: Family slopes & 500-space parking
ecosystem.body: Gentle runs, sled zones, and 500 free parking spaces—right scale for a visitor's first snow. With upper runs closed, snowshoe and forest-guide reuse of unused terrain is a mid-term option.

access.title: ~30 min from Kanazawa Station · Komatsu Airport link
access.body: ~30 minutes by car from central Kanazawa. Rental car from Komatsu Airport loops Kenrokuen → Iouzan → Yuwa Onsen easily. Winter shuttle and MaaS integration are mid-term goals.

green.title: Natural park in summer, snow play in winter
green.body: Iouzan Prefectural Natural Park—hiking and camping (Iou-no-Sato) in green season, municipal skiing in winter. Build the Iouzan brand year-round.

access.note: ~30 min by car from Kanazawa Station · ~1 hour from Komatsu Airport · 500 free parking
access.places: 石川県Ishikawa金沢市Kanazawa安原町Yasuhara（医王山Mt. Iou）
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Komatsu Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Kanazawa city and Yuwa Onsen loops

footer.copyright: © Kanazawa City Iouzan Ski Resort — LP mock

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and closures are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introTitle: Snow in the morning, Kanazawa flavors at noon
nearbyFood.introLead: No on-hill dining means routing guests to local food—the heart of the half-day model.
nearbyFood.introBody: From Morinosato gyoza to Yuwa set meals and Kanazawa regional cuisine.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: Easy to chain Iouzan → Morinosato → Yuwa.

slope.title: 7th Gyoza Shop
slope.lead: Kanazawa's soul-food gyoza with huge popularity. Drop from Iouzan into Morinosato—affordable, filling lunch.

prison.title: Takao Shokudo
prison.lead: A Yuwa Onsen town staple. Warm local flavors—pairs with Edo Village and Shirasagi-no-yu.

soba.title: Iwana Chaya
soba.lead: Regional river fish and mountain vegetables—deeper Kanazawa backcountry dining after snow play.

crab.title: Cafe Lente
crab.lead: A Yuwa café for light lunch and coffee before an afternoon Edo Village stroll.

hero.title:
Yuwa Onsen—
Kanazawa's retreat.
hero.tagline: 1,300 years of hot springs after snow play

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introLead: ~15 minutes from Iouzan—the afternoon chapter of the Snow & Onsen Kanazawa half-day trip.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: Three representative Yuwa picks from day-trip baths to stays.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery after snow play and the core of Kanazawa touring.

kohanso.title: Yuwa Onsen Public Bath Shirasagi-no-yu
kohanso.lead: The symbol of Yuwa Onsen. Day-trip bathing to warm cold legs—room for lift-ticket discount tie-ins with the ski area.

kanko.title: Share Kanazawa Natural Hot Spring
kanko.lead: Natural baths in Kanazawa before returning to the city—an in-town recovery option.

station.title: Zenigame (day-trip bathing)
station.lead: A Itakaya classic. Regional cuisine plus day-trip bathing—food and hot springs together in Yuwa.

hero.title:
Sleep near Kanazawa Station,
or in Yuwa.

corridorStay.notice: Rooms and rates are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Kenrokuen and chaya districts from Kanazawa Station hotels; snow-plus-onsen focus from Yuwa lodges—choose by trip theme.

kohanso.title: Yuwa Onsen lodges
kohanso.lead: Iouzan → Yuwa → Kanazawa city next day—stay in Yuwa for a snow-and-onsen two-night model.

kanko.title: Hotels near Kanazawa Station / Korinbo
kanko.lead: Walkable to Kenrokuen and Higashi Chaya. Half-day Iouzan, Kanazawa food and culture at night—the classic choice for international visitors.

station.title: Airport → Kanazawa → Iouzan loop
station.lead: Land at Komatsu, rent a car. Two nights in Kanazawa with a half-day at Iouzan and optional Noto extension.
